snapshot: Japan's cash equity market is closed for Children's Day. JPX's holiday calendar lists May 5, 2026 as Children's Day.
At 10:47 JST, Nikkei 225 futures were 59,470, up 50 yen. Holiday derivatives are trading, but there is no cash-equity price discovery.
Reuters reported that while Japan and South Korea were closed, MSCI Asia-Pacific ex-Japan fell 0.3%, Brent was $113.85, U.S. crude was $105.03, and U.S. equity futures were slightly lower.
USD/JPY was around 157.22. Intervention caution still matters near 160, and thin holiday liquidity can amplify FX moves.
Buyback announcements from M3, DTS and Alinco after the May 1 close remain unprocessed in cash trading. Japan cash equities reopen on May 7.

drivers.driver: No same-day cash watchlist because Japan is closed
drivers.why_today: Japan cash equities are shut for Children's Day, so there is no live order book, turnover, sector-relative action or opening reaction to validate a midday long ranking.

drivers.driver: Middle East, oil and yen-intervention caution dominate the external setup
drivers.why_today: The U.S. and Iran continue to trade blows around the Strait of Hormuz, oil remains above $100, and the yen is still being watched after suspected intervention.

drivers.driver: Buyback catalysts are being deferred to the post-holiday cash session
drivers.why_today: M3, DTS, Alinco and Kyocera have shareholder-return catalysts, but the holiday means there is no same-day cash-market reaction. The first real check is the May 7 session.

headline_inventory.headline: JPX's holiday calendar lists May 5, 2026 as Children's Day, meaning Japan's domestic cash market is closed
Nikkei 225 futures were 59,470 at 10:47 JST, up 50 yen, with the trading date shown as May 7
Reuters reported that while Japan and South Korea were closed, MSCI Asia-Pacific ex-Japan fell 0.3%, Brent was $113.85 and U.S. crude was $105.03
Reuters reported S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures down about 0.1%, with European futures also lower
Reuters' FX story showed USD/JPY around 157.22 and continued intervention caution near the 160 area
JPX's derivatives holiday-trading schedule lists Children's Day 2026 as an implemented holiday-trading day
M3 announced a buyback capped at 3.00% of shares outstanding and 20 billion yen
Alinco announced a buyback capped at 5.5% of shares outstanding and 1 billion yen
DTS announced a buyback capped at 3.08% of shares outstanding and 5 billion yen, with acquired shares to be fully cancelled
Sanko Sangyo announced cancellation of treasury stock equal to 1.10% of shares outstanding
Kyocera announced a buyback capped at 11.88% of shares outstanding and 250 billion yen, plus a cancellation equal to 6.05% of shares outstanding
Nippon Gas announced cancellation of treasury shares equal to 4.3% of shares outstanding

deep_dive.title: Children's Day holiday edition: no watchlist today, only a carry-over map into May 7

lines: May 5, 2026 is Children's Day and Japan's cash market is closed, so there is no live order book, turnover or sector-relative tape to validate stock selection.
For this midday edition, the long watchlist is intentionally empty and the focus is on external inputs plus unprocessed after-close disclosures for the May 7 cash reopen.
JPX's calendar confirms the May 5 and May 6 market holidays, leaving Japan without cash-equity price discovery through this part of Golden Week.
Reuters still reported weaker Asian shares, Brent above $113, USD/JPY in the low 157s and slightly lower U.S. equity futures, so the setup can still change while cash is closed.
Into the May 7 open, the first question is whether Nikkei 225 futures around 59,470, USD/JPY in the low 157s and Brent around $113 remain stable or reprice.
The second is whether buybacks from M3, DTS, Alinco and Kyocera can become stock-specific support once cash trading resumes.
Middle East headlines, U.S. payroll expectations or major earnings could materially change futures, FX and oil assumptions while Japan is closed.
Holiday futures and FX moves should not be mapped mechanically onto individual cash equities without fresh price discovery.
A holiday edition has no same-day cash price discovery, so ranking precision is intentionally reduced.
Additional disclosures or overseas events before May 7 can change the priority of May 1 after-close materials.

no_trade_conditions: When the domestic cash market is closed and there is no same-day price discovery to validate stock selection
When holiday moves in futures, FX or crude are being copied mechanically into individual cash-equity ideas
When Middle East headlines, U.S. payroll expectations or major earnings materially alter the May 7 premise
When May 1 after-close TDnet filings are altered in substance by follow-up disclosure or company clarification before May 7
